title: 1. RNA isolation for tissue
steps: Homogenization in TriZol Add 1mL of Trizol reagent per 30mg of tissue and homogenize using handheld homogenizer
Incubate at RT for 5 mins to allow nucleoprotein complexes to dissociate
Spin down at max speed, chilled centrifuge for 15 minutes
Carefully remove the top aqueous phase and transfer to a new Eppendorf tube The interphase and bottom organic phase can be saved for DNA and protein respectively
To the aqueous phase, add 500 microliters of 100% isopropanol, mix by inversion and incubate at -20C for a minimum for 2hrs
Spin down at max speed for 30 minutes to precipitate RNA
Remove supernatant, and add 1mL of 75% Ethanol to wash the pellet
Add 1/5 the volume of trizol of chloroform carefully, and vortex to mix well
Spin down at max speed for 15 minutes and remove supernatant
Resuspend pellet in appropriate volume of nuclease free water
creator: Sze-Xian Lim
